ops.body:
## The partition

`~/.getaway/preferences.json` holds only always-true profile facts: `op_ref`, `home_airport`, `origin_airports`, `avoid_transit`, `avoid_airlines`, `statuses`, `balances`, `learnings`. Trip-scoped constraints — cabin, date window, party, regions, vibe, destination avoids — model facts that vary per journey and live in `~/.getaway/plans/<slug>.json` (managed by `plan-new`/`plan-set`/`plan-show`/`plan-list`/`plan-done`, active pointer in `plans/current`).

## Why the split exists

The user ruled (2026-07-11) that cabin/trip_length_days/departure_days/avoid_destinations were never global preferences — they leaked out of specific trip asks. The sharpest case: avoiding Seoul/Tokyo as *final destinations* for one trip must not become a global veto, because those airports remain first-rate connections; a global `avoid_destinations` confused subagents into dropping routings through ICN/NRT. Hence the trip field is named `avoid_final_destinations` (final-destination-only; connections/layovers/positioning stay valid) and the only global geographic veto is `avoid_transit` (never connect through, e.g. "never route me through IST").

## Routing rules

- Constraints write to the plan file via `plan-set` the moment they're pinned down mid-planning, so resumed sessions and subagents read them from disk.
- Facts phrased as always-true ("I never fly X", a balance correction) go to `prefs-set` immediately.
- Stop-hook reflection (`hooks/reflect.py`) routes three ways: always-true → prefs-set; trip-scoped → the active plan via plan-set (skipped when `plans/current` is absent); skill/API corrections → repo doc edit or `~/.getaway/learnings.md`.

## Invariants when touching the schemas

- Never re-add a trip-shaped key to the global template; `prefs-set` rejects unknown patch keys AND merged documents carrying keys absent from the template.
- `plan-set` refuses `slug`/`created` (stamped by `plan-new` only).
- No version fields, no migrations, no back-compat readers: validation is shape-only against the shipped templates, and a file that doesn't fit is re-initialized, not migrated (user ruling, 2026-07-11).
- `trip <availability-ID>` is the seats.aero expansion command — the trip-memory surface is `plan-*`, never `trip-*`.
